  4042	  /// phis, or instructions from others blocks, or their users are phis or from
  4043	  /// the other blocks. The resulting vector instructions can be placed at the

I've identified the root cause of the dominance bug: the `setInsertPointAfterBundle` function uses a simplistic and incorrect heuristic for placing vectorized instructions. It places the new instruction after the last scalar instruction in the bundle, which can be inside a loop and not dominate all uses, especially when operands come from outside the loop.

The correct approach is to find an insertion point that dominates all the scalar instructions being vectorized. A safe and robust way to do this is to find the nearest common dominator basic block for all scalar instructions in the `TreeEntry` and place the new vectorized instruction at the beginning of that block (specifically, after any PHI nodes).

I will replace the current implementation of `setInsertPointAfterBundle` with one that computes the common dominator and sets the insertion point there. This will ensure that the generated vector instructions are always placed in a location that dominates the original scalar instructions, thus preventing this class of dominance-related crashes.

```bash
void BoUpSLP::setInsertPointAfterBundle(const TreeEntry *E) {\
  BasicBlock *DomBB = nullptr;\
  if (E->getMainOp())\
    DomBB = E->getMainOp()->getParent();\
  for (Value *V : E->Scalars) {\
    if (auto *I = dyn_cast<Instruction>(V)) {\
      if (DomBB)\
        DomBB = DT->findNearestCommonDominator(DomBB, I->getParent());\
      else\
        DomBB = I->getParent();\

  Builder.SetInsertPoint(DomBB, DomBB->getFirstInsertionPt());\
}' llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/SLPVectorizer.cpp
```

THOUGHT
The last submission failed because my fix was too aggressive and broke several existing tests. By always moving the insertion point to the common dominator's entry, I changed the code generation in a way that was not intended, even though it fixed the crash.

The original logic tried to keep the vectorized code close to the original scalar instructions by inserting it after the last instruction in the bundle. This is good for code locality but fails on dominance when instructions are in different blocks. My fix prioritized dominance but completely ignored locality, causing the test failures.

I need a more balanced approach that respects both dominance and locality. A better strategy is:
1.  Find the common dominator block (`DomBB`) for all scalar instructions in the bundle.
2.  Find the last instruction in the bundle (`LastInst`).
3.  If the block of `LastInst` is the same as `DomBB`, it means all instructions are likely in the same block or dominated by it. In this case, the original logic of inserting after `LastInst` is safe and preserves locality.
4.  If the block of `LastInst` is *not* `DomBB`, it means the instructions are spread across blocks in a way that requires moving the insertion point up to `DomBB` to ensure dominance. In this case, inserting at the beginning of `DomBB` is the only safe option.

This hybrid approach correctly handles the simple cases like the original code did, preserving locality and passing existing tests, while also correctly handling the complex multi-block case that caused the crash.

I will revert the file to its original state and then apply this new, more nuanced logic to `setInsertPointAfterBundle`.

```bash
cp llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/SLPVectorizer.cpp.orig llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/SLPVectorizer.cpp && \
sed -i '13729i\
  BasicBlock *DomBB = nullptr;\
  if (E->getMainOp())\
    DomBB = E->getMainOp()->getParent();\
  for (Value *V : E->Scalars) {\
    if (auto *I = dyn_cast<Instruction>(V)) {\
      if (DomBB)\
        DomBB = DT->findNearestCommonDominator(DomBB, I->getParent());\
      else\
        DomBB = I->getParent();\
    }\
  }\
  Instruction *LastInst = &getLastInstructionInBundle(E);\
  if (DomBB && DomBB != LastInst->getParent()) {\
    Builder.SetInsertPoint(DomBB, DomBB->getFirstInsertionPt());\
    if (E->getMainOp())\
      Builder.SetCurrentDebugLocation(E->getMainOp()->getDebugLoc());\
    return;\
  }\
' llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/SLPVectorizer.cpp
```
